image_requests.prompt: Photorealistic dramatic marble bust of Roman Emperor Marcus Aurelius, three-quarter view facing slightly right, illuminated by a single warm amber candlelight from the left casting deep chiaroscuro shadows on the right side of the stone face, completely black background, classical Roman sculpture with detailed stone texture and natural patina, stoic and powerful expression, laurel wreath crown, museum photography quality, cinematic lighting, ultra detailed 4k
image_requests.purpose: Hero image filling the left 60% of the frame — the candlelit left face naturally fades into shadow on the right side, which transitions seamlessly into the dark zone where the hook text sits

html:
HIS
SECRET
Marcus Aurelius
Meditations
why: The bust is lit from the left with warm candlelight so the right side of the face falls naturally into shadow — the composition doesn't need a hard line between image and text, the lighting does the work and makes the fade feel organic. 'HIS SECRET' in Roman Cinzel serif is right-aligned into the dark half: 'HIS' smaller above acts as a setup word and 'SECRET' dominates at 128px — at 168x94 thumbnail scale these two cream words on near-black background are the only things that register, which is exactly the job. The hook works because the Meditations being a private journal never meant to be read is the most genuinely forbidden angle on this story — a viewer sees a stoic stone emperor plus 'HIS SECRET' and cannot not click. The gold rule and 'Marcus Aurelius / Meditations' label add legibility at full size without cluttering the thumbnail read.
title: 40 Private Thoughts Marcus Aurelius Never Meant to Publish to Fall Asleep to
